urgency.message: 89 critical CVE(s) and 5 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: 25-Year-Old Vulnerability Patched in Curl
items.summary:
The latest version of the open source data transfer tool resolves 18 medium and low-severity vulnerabilities.
The post 25-Year-Old Vulnerability Patched in Curl appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Mistic Backdoor Linked to KongTuke in ClickFix and ModeloRAT Campaigns
items.summary:
A new, stealthy backdoor named Mistic has been deployed as part of suspected financially motivated attacks aimed at multiple organizations spanning insurance, education, IT, and professional services sectors since April 2026.

According to Symantec and Carbon Black's Threat Hunter Team, the backdoor

items.title: NIST Opens Updated IoT Security Guidance to Public Review
items.summary:
The guidance aims to establish product cybersecurity requirements for IoT devices integrated into federal agencies’ networks.
The post NIST Opens Updated IoT Security Guidance to Public Review appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Chrome 149 Update Resolves 18 Severe Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
More than half of the bugs are use-after-free defects, which can potentially lead to remote code execution.
The post Chrome 149 Update Resolves 18 Severe Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited Months Before Patching
items.summary:
CVE-2026-20245, the 7th Cisco SD-WAN vulnerability exploited in 2026, was used for months prior to its disclosure and patching.
The post Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited Months Before Patching appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Zero-Day CVE-2026-20245 Exploited to Gain Root Access
items.summary:
An unknown threat actor exploited a recently disclosed high-severity security flaw impacting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN as a zero-day at least two months before it was publicly disclosed, according to new findings from Google-owned Mandiant.

The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20245 (CVSS score: 7.8)

items.title: Google releases new privacy controls for activity history, personalization
items.summary: Google is rolling out new privacy controls for Search services and Google Play, giving you more control over saved history and personalized recommendations. [...]

items.title: DraftKings hacker 'Snoopy' sentenced to 18 months in prison
items.summary: A 21-year-old using the alias "Snoopy" was sentenced to 18 months in prison for his role in hacking DraftKings accounts in the November 2022 cyberattack. [...]

items.title: Mandiant reveals how Cisco SD-WAN zero-day attacks gained root access
items.summary: New details have been revealed on how hackers exploited a Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-20245 in zero-day attacks to create rogue root accounts on targeted devices. [...]

items.title: Attackers Hit Cisco SD-WAN Flaw 2 Months Before Disclosure
items.summary: Researchers believe rogue peering was used to connect to the victim's SD-WAN devices to gain admin privileges and root-level access.

items.title: Malicious Edge extension abuses Native Messaging as bridge to malware
items.summary: A malicious Microsoft Edge extension dubbed 'Edgecution' has been used in a ransomware attack to escape the browser sandbox and deploy a Python-based backdoor. [...]

items.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Faces Surge in Cyber Threats
items.summary: Persistent cybercrime, social engineering, and infrastructure threats continue to plague the FIFA 2026 World Cup across the US, Canada, and Mexico.

items.title: Three ‘cybercrime as a service’ operations undercut by Microsoft, law enforcement
items.summary: Microsoft touted its latest action against malware infrastructure as a new approach aimed at the full cybercrime "supply chain." Europol said more than 300 servers were targeted.

items.title: When Information Becomes the Attack Surface – Understanding AI Agent Traps
items.summary:
From hidden content injections to cognitive state poisoning, attackers are turning trusted data sources into traps for autonomous AI.
The post When Information Becomes the Attack Surface – Understanding AI Agent Traps appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Warns Critical Lantronix EDS5000 Flaw Is Being Actively Exploited
items.summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday warned of active exploitation of a critical security flaw impacting Lantronix EDS5000 Series devices, urging Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the fixes by June 26, 2026.

items.title: More Malicious OpenClaw Skills Threaten AI Supply Chain
items.summary: OpenClaw removed five packages from ClawHub, its skills marketplace, that bypassed security checks even though they included infostealers and other threats.

items.title: Researchers Trick AI Browsers Into Leaking Credentials
items.summary: LayerX tricked AI browsers including ChatGPT Atlas and Comet into bypassing their guardrails

items.title: Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered
items.summary:
A coordinated law enforcement operation, in partnership with private sector companies, including Bitdefender, Bitsight, ESET, and Microsoft, has resulted in the takedown of criminal infrastructure powering Amadey and StealC.

items.summary: Operation Endgame seized around 50 domains and nearly 200 active IP-based servers associated with the infostealers

items.title: Microsoft and Allies Smash Shared Infrastructure of Amadey and StealC Malware
items.summary:
Hundreds of C&C servers were disrupted in an operation involving law enforcement and several cybersecurity companies.

items.title: CISA warns of max severity Ubiquiti flaws exploited in attacks
items.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning of hackers actively exploiting flaws in Ubiquity UniFi OS and Lantronix serial-to-ethernet servers. [...]

items.title: Amadey, StealC malware operations disrupted in Operation Endgame action
items.summary: Microsoft, Europol, and international partners have disrupted infrastructure used by the Amadey and StealC malware operations as part of Operation Endgame, which targets cybercriminal services and ransomware gangs. [...]

items.title: Exclusive: Meet AIVEX, a New Triage Model Built to Reduce Supply Chain Threat and Risk
items.summary:

items.title: German rail services resume after wireless communications outage
items.summary: Deutsche Bahn said a nationwide disruption of railway services was tied to a malfunction in its 2G-based GSM-R communications system.

items.title: Securing the service desk: Why social engineering attacks keep succeeding
items.summary: Service desks have become a favored target for attackers seeking password resets, MFA changes, and access to corporate accounts. Specops Software breaks down how service desk social engineering attacks work and how organizations can defend against them. [...]

items.title: macOS Backdoor Uses Prompt Injection to Evade AI Triage
items.summary: SentinelLabs found a North Korea-linked macOS backdoor using prompt injection on AI triage tools

items.title: macOS Weaknesses Chained to Silently Disable Endpoint Security Agents
items.summary:
A standard non-admin account is sufficient to conduct an attack that exploits legitimate OS behavior rather than software vulnerabilities.
The post macOS Weaknesses Chained to Silently Disable Endpoint Security Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Indian auto giant Bajaj Auto hit by ransomware incident
items.summary: The company said in a regulatory filing that it became aware of the incident on Tuesday morning and had taken precautionary measures to contain its impact.

items.title: How much cyber risk does AI create for organizations? 457 million security issues. Here’s what you can do about it.
items.summary: Over a 30 day period, Tenable detected 457 million AI-related security issues among 7,000-plus organizations, an average of 62,000 exposures per organization. items.title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new class of CI/CD workflow weakness that allows attackers to hijack workflows and compromise open-source supply chains.

The "critical exploitable pattern" has been codenamed Cordyceps by Novee Security. The issue can allow full attacker control of repositor

items.title: KDDI Breach Affects Six Japanese ISPs, Exposes 14.2 Email Credentials
items.summary: Customers of the affected Japanese email services are “strongly advised” to change their email passwords

items.title: Apple's MacOS Gap Lets Users Disable Security Tools
items.summary: Attackers can exploit the issue to disable security and integrated browser tools without needing administrator privileges or kernel exploits.

items.title: Iran-Linked MuddyWater Poses as Ransomware Gang to Mask Cyber Espionage
items.summary: An NCC Group report warns state-backed hackers are attempting to hide activity by posing as ransomware groups and deploying commercially available malware

items.title: Dawn of the Apex Agentic Adversary
items.summary:
We are standing at the end of an era we never thought to mourn: the era of human-speed threats.

For years, cybersecurity moved to a rhythm organizations could follow. A researcher found a bug, a CVE was cataloged, a vendor navigated a patch cycle, and weeks or even months later, a fix was deployed.
